title: What's a security payment (Deferred Payout)?
bodyHtml:
Let's dive deeper by addressing your questions!

 

Warning: This article only addresses security payments related to sellers' sales activities on the marketplace. security payments may also apply to their trade-in activities, which are not covered in this article.

For more information on the trade-in security payment, please refer to the following article:

 

Learn more about Trade-in security payment.


Jump to:

 

  1. What is the security payment?
  2. What impact does security payment have on sellers?
  3. How is the amount of the security payment amount defined and how does it vary?
  4. What about Callouts?
  5. When will the security payment deposit be released after an exit?
  6. Where can I consult the amount of the security payment? 

 

What is the security payment?

To begin, let's define what security payment (also referred as “Deferred Payout”) means in the context of your partnership with Back Market.

As per Article 17 of the Sellers T&Cs, the security payment is a sum of money that sellers agree to be held in their  wallet, aiming at guaranteeing customers’ rights, in particular as a result of legal guarantees and commercial warranties, cancellation rights, etc. 

 

When sellers refund customers, Back Market processes the refund on their behalf and deducts the amounts from the seller’s wallet, as it appears on the invoices (consult the My invoice step by step article for more information). Therefore, sellers must have sufficient funds available to cover any issues that may arise with the products under warranty.

 

What impact does security payment have on sellers?

There are two key implications to consider:

 

  • Predictability: you can expect that at least 95% of a seller’s wallet balance is available at each payment due date, unless you're impacted by a Callout (see section 4 below for more details);
  • Cash Flow Improvement: The higher the product quality, the lower the amount held as security payment. This quality improvement frees up more funds to reinvest in new inventory, creating a virtuous circle for sellers' business and product quality.

 

How is the amount of the security payment amount defined and how does it vary?

The security payment amount is progressively adjusted for each seller, based on (i) the estimate of their future refunds, and (ii) their tier.

 

⇒ Estimated Future Refunds amount: This calculation relies on machine learning technology developed by Back Market to predict the amount that will be refunded to customers by a seller under legal and commercial warranties. It considers the following factors:

 

  • Sales and Trade-in performance and quantity: you are expected to sell in a steady way and avoid sudden drops in sales,
  • Quality performance(consult the How do quality KPIs work? article for more information),
  • Risk assessment: utilizing financial data obtained from independent databases,
  • Refunds rate:
    • the number and frequency of claims;
    • the quality of the Seller’s processing of requests and complaints, including the rate of refunds made in respect of complaints.
  • Amount of GMV under warranty:sum of prices of products with ongoing warranty.

 

⇒ Tiering: we apply a tiering to each seller : 

 

  • Tier 1: All sellers start at this tier. The security payment is set to cover 100% of the estimated future refunds amount, while ensuring that 95% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 2: After six months of activity with Back Market,if a seller achieves revenue exceeding €30,000, maintains a defective items rate below 7% and a refunded items rate below 12%, the security payment amount is progressively reduced. It will then cover 50% of the estimated future refunds amount, while ensuring that 95% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 3: After six months of activity with Back Market, if a seller achieves revenue exceeding €30,000, maintains a defective items rate below 5% and a refund rate below 10%, the security payment amount is further reduced. It will then cover  25% of the estimated future refunds, with 98% of the wallet amount remains available to the seller at each payment due date (unless you're impacted by a Callout - see section 4 below);
  • Tier 4: After two years of activity on Back Market, if a seller maintains a defective items rate below 5% and a refunded items rate below 10% over the last 6 months, and if they have achieved commissioned GMV of more than €500,000 per month over the last 3 months, then they may be exempt from security payment.

 

Tiers are reviewed and updated quarterly. 

 

What about Callouts?

A Callout triggers a reevaluation of the security payment to cover the assessed risk level. In practical terms, this means that following a Callout, the seller's wallet amount may be fully retained as security deposit. The security deposit may also be retained over several consecutive payment due dates to cover the risk level. 

 

It occurs if any of the following event happens:

 

  • Significant decrease in sales or in the number of orders fulfilled
  • Detection of atypical behaviors (such as high rate of declaration of OOW or of transportation issues)
  • Fraud detection
  • Counterfeit alert
  • Safety alert
  • Quality performance issues (see How do quality KPIs work?), including Defective rate 30 days >9%
  • Significant discrepancy between security payment amount and estimated future refunds after the Launch Phase
  • Exit / Seller Account is closed

 

If two Callouts occur within 30 days, the seller’s Tier may be downgraded to Tier 1.

 

Regarding the release of funds after an alert is lifted:

 

  • If a seller receives an alert but remains active on the marketplace, funds are retained as long as the risk persists. Once the alert is lifted (for example: fraud suspicion lifted, counterfeit report resolved followed by three months without new incident, return to acceptable service quality, etc.), the exceptional security payment is progressively released. This release is carried out at 10% of the total security payment amount at each payment due date, until the security payment amount returns to its usual level, in accordance with section 3 above. However, a seller who has received two alerts over a 30-day period may be downgraded to Tier 1 so that the release is then carried out within the limit of the estimated future refunds amount.
  • If a seller receives an alert when they are no longer active on the marketplace, the release conditions for the exceptional security payment are detailed in section 5 below.

 

When will the security payment deposit be released after an exit?

If a seller account is closed for any reason, payment due dates following the closure will be blocked as security deposit, up to a maximum amount of 1.5 times the estimated future refunds. This measure manages the increased risks of returns and warranty claims related to account closure.

 

For a period of six months following the closure of your account, during which most refunds and repairs occur, the security payment will be used to cover the costs of repairs, replacements, or refunds to customers.

After this six-month period, any remaining balance of the security payment that has not yet been used will be released in increments of 10%, with the full remaining amount being released 13 months after your account is closed (or 24 months for sales to buyers located in Spain and Portugal).

 

As an exception, if your security payment is 2,000€/£/$ or less, the incremental payment will not apply, and the balance will be refunded to you in full in a single payment 13 months after your account is closed (or 24 months if sales have been made to buyers located in Spain and Portugal).

 

Where can I consult the amount of the security payment? 

There are two ways of consulting your security payment amount:

 

  • Back Office: by going to the ‘Money’ section and then to the 'Your wallet' subsection. The security payment amount is displayed on the right side.
  • Invoice (line “Sub-Total current net financial deposit”) as for the below screenshot:


 

In case you are active in Trade-in and have exposure to Back Market, please note that the security payment amount might include a Trade-in security payment. Our team is working to ensure that the Trade-in security payment amount is visible separately from your security payment (Sales). This feature will be available as soon as possible.

 

Learn more about Trade-in security payment


For any questions regarding the security payment after your account is closed, you can contact legal-sellers@backmarket.com
